data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0027-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — T12 / `AJC.picrep` (after run 0010 r2 reconcile)

- Useful context: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean` builds green (8558 jobs), **5 sorries**, all TRUE: `genericallyFree_quotient_prime` (441), `genericFlatness` (577), `flatLocusStratification` (631), `flatLocusReduction` (660), `flatteningStratification_universal` (808). The `GenericFreeness` section (250–491) is a complete axiom-clean dévissage; only the Noether-normalisation core `genericallyFree_quotient_prime` (Stacks 051R special case) stays open. Blueprint `lem:generically_free_domain_core` in `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` carries the full Nitsure proof; Lean plan is in the core's docstring and `I-0092`.

- Relevant files/decls for the wider cone: `QuotScheme.lean` (8 sorries — 5 headline reps decls + 3 flatness-gated `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_*`), `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` (4 stubs, gated on the Quot engine). The 3 QuotScheme base-change leaves are unblocked only once T2/`AJC.fbc` (Stacks 02KH) lands; the 5 headline decls need a Route-C coherent-χ / Riemann–Roch substrate. Triage in `I-0086`; QuotScheme Lane-F state in `I-0089`.

- Recent work: Horizon repaired three false pinned statements in FlatteningStratification (quasi-compactness, flat-over-stratum, bundled universal property) and built the dévissage layer; Ground verified the counterexamples and build. `flatLocusReduction` (Noetherian induction on the fixed `genericFlatness`) and `genericFlatness`'s affine/quasi-compact glue are the reduction's next pieces. References cited: Nitsure §4 (`nitsure-hilbert-quot`), Stacks 051R.

- Consistency note: blueprint sync verified honest — 27 `\leanok`, 0 `\notready`, 0 dangling refs; the sorry-core node has statement-`\leanok` only. `I-0087` (12 dangling QuotScheme union-merge labels) is closed; residual node-less helpers (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback`, Piece-A `isQuasicoherent_pullback_of_isOpenImmersion`) and the cross-file duplicate label `lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib` remain for a future blueprint polish pass.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

- Consequence: a blueprint node whose only dependencies are declared inside its proof block shows as **isolated** in `horizon leandag`, even though the math dependency is real and the proof is complete. This is expected, not a blueprint defect.
- Observed run 0006 (Albanese): 297 nodes, **178 isolated**, but **0 dangling** and 287 proved — the high isolated count is entirely this artifact.
- **The health metric to trust is `dangling` (should be 0) and `proved`, NOT `isolated`.** Do not chase isolated nodes as missing edges; if you want them linked in the DAG, lift the relevant `\uses` from the proof block up to the statement line.
- Same engine limitation was noted by the T6 Horizon session; recorded here so future Ground audits don't misread it.
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **Stacks 01BG general pullback-qcoh CLOSED + FBC RHS tilde leaf CLOSED (run 0003 session 0013, T2) — supersedes all "general-morphism pullback qcoh is absent / only in sorry-tainted QuotScheme" notes.**

- `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (new, ~185 LOC, axiom-clean): `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (g : Y ⟶ X)` for ARBITRARY scheme morphisms. Recipe: localize the pullback, not the presentation — per cover member `A`, `(g^*F)|_{g⁻¹A} ≅ (g.resLE A W)^*(F|_A)` via `pullbackComp`+`pullbackCongr (resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)`+`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, transport the global presentation of `F|_A` by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` (15-underscore @-application, copy `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver`'s shape), then the CechTermAcyclic over-bridge tail (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence` / `overOpensIsoRestrict` / `overOpensFunctorUnitIso` / counit) and `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover.
- Enablers now available: `opensMap_final` (`(Opens.map φ).Final` for ANY continuous map — structured-arrow posets are ⊔-directed, `Functor.final_of_isFiltered_structuredArrow`) and `pullbackUnitIso : g^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` (Mathlib's `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso under `[F.Final]`). Gotcha: re-synthesis of `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)` at USE sites can fail (defeq wall in the instance-arg unification) — pass the instance explicitly via `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom g)`.
- FBC leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` (622-line sorry) closed as literally `(pushforward f').mapIso (twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma …) ≪≫ pushPullObj_coverInter_pushforward_iso_tilde f' 𝒰' (g'^*F) (pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g' F hF) σ` — the def had to MOVE below `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` in the file. FBC leaves 5 → 4.
- Iteration cost: `lake env lean` on a file importing Mathlib+`CechTermAcyclic` ≈ 4 min under load ~65; the 2-module `lake build` (new file + `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) ≈ 25 min.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.

Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. In that section, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

I-0092 open memory
**Recipes: module-level dévissage in Lean v4.31 (landed in FlatteningStratification GenericFreeness, run 0010 T12 r2) — reuse for any prime-filtration/SES argument.**

- **ModuleCat SES arguments need ONE universe**: `ModuleCat.ofHom {X Y : Type v}` forces all modules of a `ShortComplex (ModuleCat R)` into the same universe. Binders `{M₁ M₂ M₃ : Type*}` give distinct universes and produce baffling failures — instance goals displayed as raw `Quot ⇑(OreLocalization.oreEqv ...)` (LocalizedModule unfolded past its instance heads) plus universe-mismatched `Eq.{max u_1 u_4}` vs `Eq.{max u_1 u_2}`. Pin `Type v`. The dévissage eliminator is single-universe anyway.
- **`set S := Submonoid.powers (f*g)` breaks instance unification** in localized-module types (same family as the known let-fvar IsIso gotcha): instance search will not zeta-unfold the set-fvar when unifying `Localization S'` heads. Write the submonoid term explicitly everywhere; `let`-bound MAPS (data) are fine — only types/instances mind.
- **Quantified-instance motive** for `IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime` when the property lives over a DIFFERENT ring A than the induction ring B: `motive N _ iMB _ := ∀ (iA : Module A N), @IsScalarTower A B N Algebra.toSMul iMB.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul iA.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul → @P A N _ _ iA`. Cases: subsingleton via @-application; quotient case transports along `(e.restrictScalars A).symm` (canonical tower on B⧸p is instance-found); exact case builds `letI iA₁ := Module.compHom N₁ (algebraMap A B)` + tower `⟨fun a b n => by change (a • b) • n = algebraMap A B a • b • n; rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]⟩`; B-linear maps restrict via `LinearMap.restrictScalars A` and inj/surj/Exact transfer definitionally. Final application `... ‹Module A M› ‹IsScalarTower A B M›` unifies. Compiled essentially first-try.
- **Two-localization freeness transport** (`M_f` free ⟹ `M_{fg}` free) WITHOUT IsLocalizedModule-composition: `(IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight f g).toAlgebra` + `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (awayToAwayRight_eq ..).symm`, then equiv-chain `congr(refl, isBaseChange f) .symm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ isBaseChange (f*g)` and `Module.Free.tensor` + `Module.Free.of_equiv'`. Give every LinearEquiv a fully-typed `have` — `refine LinearEquiv.trans ?_ e` with open source type sticks on RingHomCompTriple metavariables.
- **Localized SES**: `IsLocalizedModule.map/map_injective/map_surjective/map_exact` at `mkLinearMap`s, upgrade R-linear→Loc-linear with `LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization` (coe unchanged, so Function.Exact/Injective/Surjective transfer by defeq); `ShortComplex.mk (ofHom u') (ofHom v') (ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun x => hexact.apply_apply_eq_zero x))`; ShortExact via `moduleCat_exact_iff` + `mono_iff_injective` + `epi_iff_surjective`; finish `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`.
- **refine-under-binder**: `refine subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 fun x => ?_` fails (?_ under lambda); use `apply` + `intro` + `induction x using LocalizedModule.induction_on with | _ m s => ...`. `Submonoid.mk_smul` bridges `(⟨c,hc⟩ : ↥S) • m` to `c • m`.

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (run 0010, T12 r2 — statement repair + dévissage layer)
19	
20	The iter-176 file-skeleton carried three FALSE pinned statements, repaired
21	this round (see the per-declaration docstrings for the counterexamples):
22	`genericFlatness` gained `[QuasiCompact p]` (finite type = qc + locally of
23	finite type; `⨿_q Spec 𝔽_q → Spec ℤ` refuted the old form),
24	`flatLocusStratification` now asserts flatness over the *stratum*
25	(`CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (S_ e))`; flatness over ambient `S` fails for any
26	closed stratum), and `flatteningStratification_universal` now *bundles*
27	existence with the coproduct-factorisation universal property (the old
28	form quantified over arbitrary immersion families, refuted by `I = PEmpty`).
29	
30	Proof state: `flatteningStratification` and `flatLocusAssembly` are proved
31	from `flatLocusReduction` (their typed content coincides with Lemma 6 up
32	to conjunct order / a choice of injection). The `GenericFreeness` section
33	now contains the full *dévissage layer* of Nitsure's algebraic
34	generic-freeness lemma — splicing under short exact sequences, torsion
35	base case, localization transport, and the prime-filtration assembly
36	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` — with the Noether-normalisation domain core
37	`genericallyFree_quotient_prime` as its single remaining `sorry`.
38	
39	Remaining `sorry`s (5, all TRUE statements): `genericallyFree_quotient_prime`,
40	`genericFlatness`, `flatLocusStratification`, `flatLocusReduction`,
41	`flatteningStratification_universal`.
42	
43	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
46	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
47	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
48	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
49	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
50	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
51	   affine-local descent characterisation.
52	
53	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
54	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
55	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
56	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
57	
58	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
59	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
60	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
61	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
62	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
63	   stratum.
64	
65	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
66	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
67	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
68	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
69	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
70	
71	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
72	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
73	
74	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
75	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
76	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
77	   strata.
78	
79	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
80	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
81	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
82	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
83	
84	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
85	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
86	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
87	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
88	   stratification.
89	
90	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
91	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
92	   `C ×_k T → T`.
93	
94	## Note on type expressivity
95	
96	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
97	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
98	
99	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
100	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
101	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
102	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
103	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
104	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
105	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
106	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
107	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
108	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
109	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
110	  one trivialises if dropped).
111	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
112	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
113	  bijection content.
114	
115	## Mathlib status
116	
117	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`,
118	`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat` (morphism-level), `IsImmersion`,
119	`IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`. It does NOT
120	provide: a relative projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, an `IsProjective` morphism

240	(`M = B/𝔭`: Noether normalisation over `Frac A` + clearing denominators +
241	the generic-rank exact sequence + induction on fibre dimension). This
242	section proves the dévissage layer completely — the splicing lemma
243	`genericallyFree_of_exact` (via `ModuleCat.free_shortExact` and the
244	localization-transport lemma `free_localizedModule_powers_mul`), the torsion
245	base case `genericallyFree_of_torsion` (Nitsure's `n = -1`), and the
246	assembly `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` via
247	`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime` — leaving the
248	domain core `genericallyFree_quotient_prime` as the single typed `sorry`. -/
249	
250	/-- The **generic-freeness property** of an `A`-module `M`: some localization
251	`M_f` at a single non-zero `f ∈ A` is a free module over `A_f`. This is the
252	conclusion shared by every statement of this section. -/
253	def GenericallyFree (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [AddCommGroup M]
254	    [Module A M] : Prop :=
255	  ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
256	    Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)
257	
258	/-- Generic freeness transports along enlarging the inverted element: if `M_f`
259	is free over `A_f` then `M_{fg}` is free over `A_{fg}` for every `g`. The
260	proof is base-change associativity
261	`A_{fg} ⊗_{A_f} (A_f ⊗_A M) ≅ A_{fg} ⊗_A M` together with freeness of base
262	change (`Module.Free.tensor`); the two tensor legs are identified with the
263	localized modules by `IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange`. -/
264	theorem free_localizedModule_powers_mul (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A]
265	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] (f g : A)
266	    (hf : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
267	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)) :
268	    Module.Free (Localization.Away (f * g))
269	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M) := by
270	  haveI := hf
271	  letI : Algebra (Localization.Away f) (Localization.Away (f * g)) :=
272	    (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight (S := Localization.Away f)
273	      (P := Localization.Away (f * g)) f g).toAlgebra
274	  haveI : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f) (Localization.Away (f * g)) :=
275	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun a =>
276	      (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight_eq (S := Localization.Away f)
277	        (P := Localization.Away (f * g)) f g a).symm
278	  have e_f : (Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[Localization.Away f]
279	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M :=
280	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
281	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M)).equiv
282	  have e_fg : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
283	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M :=
284	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (f * g))
285	      (Localization.Away (f * g))
286	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M)).equiv
287	  have e_cancel : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
288	      ((Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
289	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[A] M :=
290	    TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange A (Localization.Away f)
291	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) (Localization.Away (f * g)) M
292	  have e_congr : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
293	      ((Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
294	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
295	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) :=
296	    TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.congr
297	      (LinearEquiv.refl (Localization.Away (f * g)) (Localization.Away (f * g))) e_f
298	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv'
299	    (inferInstance : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f * g))
300	      ((Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
301	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)))
302	    (e_congr.symm.trans (e_cancel.trans e_fg))
303	
304	/-- Generic freeness is invariant under `A`-linear equivalence. -/
305	theorem GenericallyFree.of_linearEquiv {A : Type*} [CommRing A] {M N : Type*}
306	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N]
307	    (e : M ≃ₗ[A] N) (h : GenericallyFree A M) : GenericallyFree A N := by
308	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := h
309	  refine ⟨f, hf, ?_⟩
310	  haveI := hfree
311	  have e' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M ≃ₗ[A]
312	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N :=
313	    IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers f)
314	      ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) N) ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap)
315	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv' hfree
316	    (e'.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f))
317	
318	/-- **Splicing lemma for generic freeness** — the extension step of Nitsure's
319	dévissage. If the outer terms of a short exact sequence
320	`0 → M₁ → M₂ → M₃ → 0` of `A`-modules are generically free, so is the middle
321	term: localize the sequence at the product of the two witnesses (exactness of
322	localization) and apply `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`. -/
323	theorem genericallyFree_of_exact {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
324	    {M₁ M₂ M₃ : Type v} [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module A M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂]
325	    [Module A M₂] [AddCommGroup M₃] [Module A M₃]
326	    (u : M₁ →ₗ[A] M₂) (v : M₂ →ₗ[A] M₃) (hu : Function.Injective u)
327	    (hv : Function.Surjective v) (huv : Function.Exact u v)
328	    (h₁ : GenericallyFree A M₁) (h₃ : GenericallyFree A M₃) :
329	    GenericallyFree A M₂ := by
330	  obtain ⟨f₁, hf₁, hfree₁⟩ := h₁
331	  obtain ⟨f₃, hf₃, hfree₃⟩ := h₃
332	  refine ⟨f₁ * f₃, mul_ne_zero hf₁ hf₃, ?_⟩
333	  have hfree₁' := free_localizedModule_powers_mul A M₁ f₁ f₃ hfree₁
334	  have hfree₃' := free_localizedModule_powers_mul A M₃ f₃ f₁ hfree₃
335	  rw [mul_comm f₃ f₁] at hfree₃'
336	  let u' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁
337	      →ₗ[Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)]
338	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂ :=
339	    (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
340	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁)
341	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂)
342	      u).extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
343	        (Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃))
344	  let v' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂
345	      →ₗ[Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)]
346	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₃ :=
347	    (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
348	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂)
349	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₃)
350	      v).extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
351	        (Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃))
352	  have hu' : Function.Injective u' :=
353	    IsLocalizedModule.map_injective (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
354	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁)
355	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂) u hu
356	  have hv' : Function.Surjective v' :=
357	    IsLocalizedModule.map_surjective (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
358	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂)
359	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₃) v hv
360	  have huv' : Function.Exact u' v' :=
361	    IsLocalizedModule.map_exact (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
362	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁)
363	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂)
364	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₃)
365	      u v huv
366	  let C : ShortComplex (ModuleCat (Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃))) :=
367	    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom u') (ModuleCat.ofHom v')
368	      (ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun x => huv'.apply_apply_eq_zero x))
369	  have hC : C.ShortExact :=
370	    { exact := C.moduleCat_exact_iff.mpr fun x hx => (huv' x).mp hx
371	      mono_f := (ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr hu'
372	      epi_g := (ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective _).mpr hv' }
373	  haveI : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)) C.X₁ := hfree₁'
374	  haveI : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)) C.X₃ := hfree₃'
375	  exact ModuleCat.free_shortExact hC
376	
377	/-- Generic freeness of a subsingleton module (with `f = 1`). This is the
378	degenerate input to the dévissage; the substantive Nitsure base case
379	(`M_K = 0`, i.e. torsion `M`) is `genericallyFree_of_torsion` below. -/
380	theorem genericallyFree_of_subsingleton (A M : Type*) [CommRing A]
381	    [Nontrivial A] [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Subsingleton M] :
382	    GenericallyFree A M := by
383	  refine ⟨1, one_ne_zero, ?_⟩
384	  haveI : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : A)) M) := by
385	    apply subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0
386	    intro x
387	    induction x using LocalizedModule.induction_on with
388	    | _ m s =>
389	      rw [Subsingleton.elim m 0]
390	      exact LocalizedModule.zero_mk s
391	  infer_instance
392	
393	/-- **Torsion base case** of generic freeness (Nitsure's `n = -1`, `M_K = 0`):
394	a finite `A`-module all of whose elements are `A`-torsion is generically free
395	— the product of annihilators of a finite generating set kills the module,
396	so the corresponding localization vanishes. -/
397	theorem genericallyFree_of_torsion (A M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
398	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M]
399	    (htors : ∀ m : M, ∃ a : A, a ≠ 0 ∧ a • m = 0) : GenericallyFree A M := by
400	  classical
401	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := (‹Module.Finite A M› : Module.Finite A M).fg_top
402	  choose a ha haz using htors
403	  refine ⟨∏ m ∈ s, a m, Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr fun m _ => ha m, ?_⟩
404	  have hkill : ∀ m : M, (∏ m' ∈ s, a m') • m = 0 := by
405	    have hker : Submodule.span A (s : Set M) ≤
406	        LinearMap.ker (LinearMap.lsmul A M (∏ m' ∈ s, a m')) := by
407	      rw [Submodule.span_le]
408	      intro m hm
409	      simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, LinearMap.mem_ker, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]
410	      rw [← Finset.mul_prod_erase s a hm, mul_comm, mul_smul, haz m, smul_zero]
411	    intro m
412	    have hm : m ∈ Submodule.span A (s : Set M) := by
413	      rw [hs]; exact Submodule.mem_top
414	    simpa using hker hm
415	  haveI : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ m ∈ s, a m)) M) := by
416	    apply subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0
417	    intro x
418	    induction x using LocalizedModule.induction_on with
419	    | _ m t =>

421	        (⟨∏ m' ∈ s, a m', Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩ : Submonoid.powers (∏ m ∈ s, a m))
422	        t m, Submonoid.mk_smul, hkill m]
423	      exact LocalizedModule.zero_mk _
424	  infer_instance
425	
426	/-- **The domain core of algebraic generic freeness** [Nitsure §4, "Lemma on
427	Generic Flatness", normalisation step]: for a noetherian domain `A`, a
428	finite-type `A`-algebra `B` and a prime `𝔭 ⊆ B`, the quotient domain `B/𝔭`
429	is generically free over `A`.
430	
431	This is the single remaining `sorry` of the dévissage: its (future) proof is
432	Nitsure's induction on the dimension of the generic fibre — if
433	`ker (A → B/𝔭) ≠ 0` the localization vanishes (`genericallyFree_of_torsion`);
434	otherwise Noether normalisation of `Frac A ⊗_A B/𝔭` over `Frac A`
435	(`exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`), clearing denominators to make `(B/𝔭)_g`
436	module-finite over `A_g[b₁, …, b_m]` (`IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples`
437	+ `Polynomial.scaleRoots` integrality), the generic-rank short exact sequence
438	`0 → A_g[b̄]^r → (B/𝔭)_g → T → 0` with `T` torsion over the polynomial ring,
439	and the fibre-dimension drop feeding the outer induction. TRUE as stated
440	(it is a special case of Grothendieck's lemma, Stacks 051R). -/

444	  sorry
445	
446	/-- **Algebraic generic freeness, dévissage assembly** [Nitsure §4 "Lemma on
447	Generic Flatness" / Stacks 051R; blueprint `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`].
448	
449	For a noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B` and a finite
450	`B`-module `M` (with compatible `A`-structure), `M` is generically free over
451	`A`. The prime-filtration dévissage over the noetherian ring `B`
452	(`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`) is complete:
453	the subsingleton case is `genericallyFree_of_subsingleton`, prime quotients
454	are the domain core `genericallyFree_quotient_prime` (the one remaining
455	`sorry` of this section), and stability under extensions is the splicing
456	lemma `genericallyFree_of_exact`. -/
457	theorem genericFlatnessAlgebraic (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
458	    [IsNoetherianRing A] [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
459	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M] [Module A M]
460	    [IsScalarTower A B M] : GenericallyFree A M := by
461	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing B := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing A B
462	  refine IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime (A := B)
463	    ‹Module.Finite B M›
464	    (motive := fun N iACG iMB _ => ∀ (iA : Module A N),
465	      (@IsScalarTower A B N Algebra.toSMul
466	        iMB.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul
467	        iA.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul) →

470	  · intro N _ _ _ _ iA _
471	    exact @genericallyFree_of_subsingleton A N _ _ _ iA ‹_›
472	  · intro N _ _ _ p e iA htower
473	    letI := iA
474	    haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := htower
475	    exact (genericallyFree_quotient_prime A B p.1).of_linearEquiv
476	      (e.restrictScalars A).symm
477	  · intro N₁ _ _ _ N₂ _ _ _ N₃ _ _ _ u v hu hv huv h₁ h₃ iA₂ htower₂
478	    letI iA₁ : Module A N₁ := Module.compHom N₁ (algebraMap A B)
479	    letI iA₃ : Module A N₃ := Module.compHom N₃ (algebraMap A B)
480	    haveI ht₁ : IsScalarTower A B N₁ :=

483	        rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]⟩

491	      hu hv huv (h₁ iA₁ ht₁) (h₃ iA₃ ht₃)

data.content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Andrew Yang
-/
module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.FinitePresentation
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Finiteness
public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FreeModule.StrongRankCondition
public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.StrongRankCondition

/-!
# Free modules and localization

## Main result

- `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`:
  If `M` is a finitely presented `R`-module
  such that `Mₛ` is free over `Rₛ` for some `S : Submonoid R`,
  then `Mᵣ` is already free over `Rᵣ` for some `r ∈ S`.

In the file `Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.FreeLocus`, we deduce that the free
locus of a finitely presented module is open and its rank is locally constant.
-/

public section

variable {R M N N'} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
variable (S : Submonoid R) [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']

variable {M' : Type*} [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M'] (f : M →ₗ[R] M') [IsLocalizedModule S f]
variable {N' : Type*} [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] (g : N →ₗ[R] N') [IsLocalizedModule S g]

include f in
/--
If `M` is a finitely presented `R`-module,
then any `Rₛ`-basis of `Mₛ` for some `S : Submonoid R` can be lifted to
a `Rᵣ`-basis of `Mᵣ` for some `r ∈ S`.
-/
lemma Module.FinitePresentation.exists_basis_localizedModule_powers
    (Rₛ) [CommRing Rₛ] [Algebra R Rₛ] [Module Rₛ M'] [IsScalarTower R Rₛ M']
    [IsLocalization S Rₛ] [Module.FinitePresentation R M]
    {I} [Finite I] (b : Basis I Rₛ M') :
    ∃ (r : R) (hr : r ∈ S)
      (b' : Basis I (Localization (.powers r)) (LocalizedModule.Away r M)),
      ∀ i, (LocalizedModule.lift (.powers r) f fun s ↦ IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
        ⟨s.1, SetLike.le_def.mp (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr hr) s.2⟩) (b' i) = b i := by
  have : Module.FinitePresentation R (I →₀ R) := Module.finitePresentation_of_projective _ _
  obtain ⟨r, hr, e, he⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.exists_lift_equiv_of_isLocalizedModule S f
    (Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap R Rₛ)) (b.repr.restrictScalars R)
  let e' := IsLocalizedModule.iso (.powers r) (Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (α := I)
    (Algebra.linearMap R (Localization (.powers r))))
  refine ⟨r, hr, .ofRepr (e ≪≫ₗ ?_), ?_⟩
  · exact
    { __ := e',
      toLinearMap := e'.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (.powers r) (Localization (.powers r)) }
  · intro i
    have : e'.symm _ = _ := LinearMap.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp (.powers r)
      (Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap R (Localization (.powers r)))))
      (Finsupp.single i 1)
    simp only [Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap_apply, Finsupp.mapRange_single, Algebra.linearMap_apply,
      map_one, LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap_apply] at this
    change LocalizedModule.lift _ _ _ (e.symm (e'.symm _)) = _
    replace he := LinearMap.congr_fun he (e.symm (e'.symm (Finsupp.single i 1)))
    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
      Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply, LinearEquiv.restrictScalars_apply] at he
    apply b.repr.injective
    rw [← he, Basis.repr_self, this, LocalizedModule.lift_mk]
    simp

include f in
/--
If `M` is a finitely presented `R`-module
such that `Mₛ` is free over `Rₛ` for some `S : Submonoid R`,
then `Mᵣ` is already free over `Rᵣ` for some `r ∈ S`.
-/
lemma Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers
    (Rₛ) [CommRing Rₛ] [Algebra R Rₛ] [Module Rₛ M'] [IsScalarTower R Rₛ M'] [Nontrivial Rₛ]
    [IsLocalization S Rₛ] [Module.FinitePresentation R M] [Module.Free Rₛ M'] :
    ∃ r, r ∈ S ∧
      Module.Free (Localization (.powers r)) (LocalizedModule.Away r M) ∧
      Module.finrank (Localization (.powers r)) (LocalizedModule.Away r M) =
        Module.finrank Rₛ M' := by
  let I := Module.Free.ChooseBasisIndex Rₛ M'
  let b : Basis I Rₛ M' := Module.Free.chooseBasis Rₛ M'
  have : Module.Finite Rₛ M' := Module.Finite.of_isLocalizedModule S (Rₚ := Rₛ) f
  obtain ⟨r, hr, b', _⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.exists_basis_localizedModule_powers S f Rₛ b
  have := (show Localization (.powers r) →+* Rₛ from IsLocalization.map (M := .powers r) (T := S) _
    (RingHom.id _) (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr hr)).domain_nontrivial
  refine ⟨r, hr, .of_basis b', ?_⟩
  rw [Module.finrank_eq_nat_card_basis b, Module.finrank_eq_nat_card_basis b']

data.content:
(eqv2 f I).toAlgHom.comp ((eqv1 f I).toAlgHom.comp ((hom1 f I).restrictScalars k))

/-- `hom2 f I` is integral. -/
private lemma hom2_isIntegral (fne : f ≠ 0) (fi : f ∈ I) : (hom2 f I).IsIntegral :=
  ((hom1_isIntegral f I fne fi).trans _ _ <| isIntegral_of_surjective _ (eqv1 f I).surjective).trans
    _ _ <| isIntegral_of_surjective _ (eqv2 f I).surjective

end intmaps
end NoetherNormalization

section mainthm

open NoetherNormalization

/-- There exists some `s ≤ n` and an integral injective algebra homomorphism
from `k[X_0,...,X_(s-1)]` to `k[X_0,...,X_(n-1)]/I` if `I ≠ ⊤`. -/
theorem exists_integral_inj_algHom_of_quotient (I : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k))
    (hi : I ≠ ⊤) : ∃ s ≤ n, ∃ g : (MvPolynomial (Fin s) k) →ₐ[k] ((MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) ⧸ I),
    Function.Injective g ∧ g.IsIntegral := by
  induction n with
  | zero =>
    refine ⟨0, le_rfl, Quotient.mkₐ k I, fun a b hab ↦ ?_,
      isIntegral_of_surjective _ (Quotient.mkₐ_surjective k I)⟩
    rw [Quotient.mkₐ_eq_mk, Ideal.Quotient.eq] at hab
    by_contra ne
    have eq := eq_C_of_isEmpty (a - b)
    have ne : coeff 0 (a - b) ≠ 0 := fun h ↦ h ▸ eq ▸ sub_ne_zero_of_ne ne <| map_zero _
    obtain ⟨c, _, eqr⟩ := isUnit_iff_exists.mp ne.isUnit
    have one : c • (a - b) = 1 := by
      rw [MvPolynomial.smul_eq_C_mul, eq, ← map_mul, eqr, MvPolynomial.C_1]
    exact hi ((eq_top_iff_one I).mpr (one ▸ I.smul_of_tower_mem c hab))
  | succ d hd =>
    by_cases eqi : I = 0
    · have bij : Function.Bijective (Quotient.mkₐ k I) :=
        (Quotient.mk_bijective_iff_eq_bot I).mpr eqi
      exact ⟨d + 1, le_rfl, _, bij.1, isIntegral_of_surjective _ bij.2⟩
    · obtain ⟨f, fi, fne⟩ := Submodule.exists_mem_ne_zero_of_ne_bot eqi
      set ϕ := kerLiftAlg <| hom2 f I
      have := Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hi
      obtain ⟨s, _, g, injg, intg⟩ := hd (ker <| hom2 f I) (ker_ne_top <| hom2 f I)
      have comp : (kerLiftAlg (hom2 f I)).comp (Quotient.mkₐ k <| ker <| hom2 f I) = (hom2 f I) :=
        AlgHom.ext fun a ↦ by
          simp only [AlgHom.coe_comp, Quotient.mkₐ_eq_mk, Function.comp_apply, kerLiftAlg_mk]
      exact ⟨s, by lia, ϕ.comp g, (ϕ.coe_comp  g) ▸ (kerLiftAlg_injective _).comp injg,
        intg.trans _ _ <| (comp ▸ hom2_isIntegral f I fne fi).tower_top _ _⟩

variable (k R : Type*) [Field k] [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R] [a : Algebra k R]
  [fin : Algebra.FiniteType k R]

/-- **Noether normalization lemma**
For a finitely generated algebra `A` over a field `k`,
there exists a natural number `s` and an injective homomorphism
from `k[X_0, X_1, ..., X_(s-1)]` to `A` such that `A` is integral over `k[X_0, X_1, ..., X_(s-1)]`.
-/
@[stacks 00OW]
theorem exists_integral_inj_algHom_of_fg : ∃ s, ∃ g : (MvPolynomial (Fin s) k) →ₐ[k] R,
    Function.Injective g ∧ g.IsIntegral := by
  obtain ⟨n, f, fsurj⟩ := Algebra.FiniteType.iff_quotient_mvPolynomial''.mp fin
  set ϕ := quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective fsurj
  obtain ⟨s, _, g, injg, intg⟩ := exists_integral_inj_algHom_of_quotient (ker f) (ker_ne_top _)
  use s, ϕ.toAlgHom.comp g
  simp only [AlgHom.coe_comp, AlgEquiv.coe_algHom, EmbeddingLike.comp_injective,
    AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe]
  exact ⟨injg, intg.trans _ _ (isIntegral_of_surjective _ ϕ.surjective)⟩

/-- For a finitely generated algebra `A` over a field `k`,
there exists a natural number `s` and an injective homomorphism
from `k[X_0, X_1, ..., X_(s-1)]` to `A` such that `A` is finite over `k[X_0, X_1, ..., X_(s-1)]`. -/
theorem exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg : ∃ s, ∃ g : (MvPolynomial (Fin s) k) →ₐ[k] R,
    Function.Injective g ∧ g.Finite := by
  obtain ⟨s, g, ⟨inj, int⟩⟩ := exists_integral_inj_algHom_of_fg k R
  have h : algebraMap k R = g.toRingHom.comp (algebraMap k (MvPolynomial (Fin s) k)) := by
    algebraize [g.toRingHom]
    rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq k (MvPolynomial (Fin s) k), algebraMap_toAlgebra']
  exact ⟨s, g, inj, int.to_finite
    (h ▸ RingHom.finiteType_algebraMap.mpr fin).of_comp_finiteType⟩

end mainthm

148	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
149	
150	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — algebraic generic freeness (finite case)
151	
152	This section builds the **module-theoretic** generic-freeness statements that
153	underlie the geometric `genericFlatness` (blueprint
154	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness").
155	
156	The full algebraic statement — `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a *finite-type*
157	`A`-algebra, `M` a finite `B`-module ⟹ `∃ f ≠ 0` with `M_f` free over `A_f` —
158	is a deep theorem (prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
159	clearing denominators). Mathlib does **not** contain it (checked against the
160	pinned revision; only the spreading-out lemma
161	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` exists).
162	
163	This section contains, axiom-clean: the **finite-module / finite-morphism
164	case** (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`, via
165	`exists_free_localizedModule_powers` at the generic point), and — added in
166	run 0010 T12 r2 — the complete **dévissage layer** of Nitsure's proof:
167	`GenericallyFree` (the property), `free_localizedModule_powers_mul`
168	(localization transport `M_f` free ⟹ `M_{fg}` free),
169	`GenericallyFree.of_linearEquiv`, `genericallyFree_of_exact` (splicing
170	under short exact sequences, via `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`),
171	`genericallyFree_of_subsingleton`, `genericallyFree_of_torsion` (Nitsure's
172	`n = -1` base case), and the prime-filtration assembly
173	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` via
174	`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`. The single
175	remaining `sorry` is the Noether-normalisation domain core
176	`genericallyFree_quotient_prime`. -/
177	
178	namespace GenericFreeness
179	
180	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-module case.** For a noetherian integral domain
181	`A` and a finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that the
182	localisation `M_f` is free over `A_f = Localization.Away f`.
183	
184	This is the `d = 0` (finite-morphism) special case of the algebraic
185	generic-flatness theorem (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4):
186	inverting the generic point `Frac A`, the localised module is a finite vector
187	space hence free, and `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`
188	descends that freeness to a single basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec A`. -/
189	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite
190	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
191	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
192	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
193	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
194	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation A M := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite A M
195	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hfree, _⟩ :=
196	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers (nonZeroDivisors A)
197	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (nonZeroDivisors A) M) (FractionRing A)
198	  exact ⟨r, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hr, hfree⟩
199	
200	/-- **Generic flatness, finite-module case.** The flatness form of
201	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`: for a noetherian domain `A` and a
202	finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f` with `M_f` flat over `A_f`.
203	This is the affine-local content of `genericFlatness` for a finite morphism. -/
204	theorem exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite
205	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
206	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
207	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
208	      Module.Flat (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
209	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
210	  haveI := hfree
211	  exact ⟨f, hf, Module.Flat.of_free⟩
212	
213	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-morphism case.** If `A` is a noetherian domain,
214	`B` a *module-finite* `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module (with the
215	compatible `A`-module structure), then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `M_f`
216	free over `A_f`. Reduces to `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` via
217	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
218	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
219	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
220	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
221	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
222	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
223	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
224	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
225	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
226	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
227	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
228	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
229	
230	/-! ### The dévissage frame for full algebraic generic freeness
231	
232	Grothendieck's generic-freeness lemma (blueprint
233	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness"):
234	for `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra and `M` a finite
235	`B`-module there is `f ≠ 0` in `A` with `M_f` free over `A_f`.
236	
237	Nitsure's proof has two layers: a *dévissage* (prime filtration of `M` as a
238	module over the noetherian ring `B`, reducing to `M = B/𝔭`, via the splicing
239	fact that generic freeness is stable under extensions), and a *domain core*
240	(`M = B/𝔭`: Noether normalisation over `Frac A` + clearing denominators +
241	the generic-rank exact sequence + induction on fibre dimension). This
242	section proves the dévissage layer completely — the splicing lemma

data.content:
public section

open scoped nonZeroDivisors

variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]

lemma ringKrullDim_quotient (I : Ideal R) :
    ringKrullDim (R ⧸ I) = Order.krullDim (PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (R := R) I) := by
  rw [ringKrullDim, Order.krullDim_eq_of_orderIso I.primeSpectrumQuotientOrderIsoZeroLocus]

lemma ringKrullDim_quotient_succ_le_of_nonZeroDivisor
    {r : R} (hr : r ∈ R⁰) :
    ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) + 1 ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
  by_cases hr' : Ideal.span {r} = ⊤
  · rw [hr', ringKrullDim_eq_bot_of_subsingleton]
    simp
  have : Nonempty (PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (R := R) (Ideal.span {r})) := by
    rwa [Set.nonempty_coe_sort, Set.nonempty_iff_ne_empty, ne_eq,
      PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus_empty_iff_eq_top]
  have := Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hr'
  have := (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})).domain_nontrivial
  rw [ringKrullDim_quotient, Order.krullDim_eq_iSup_length, ringKrullDim,
    Order.krullDim_eq_iSup_length, ← WithBot.coe_one, ← WithBot.coe_add,
    ENat.iSup_add, WithBot.coe_le_coe, iSup_le_iff]
  intro l
  obtain ⟨p, hp, hp'⟩ := Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le (J := l.head.1.asIdeal) bot_le
  let p' : PrimeSpectrum R := ⟨p, hp.1.1⟩
  have hp' : p' < l.head := lt_of_le_of_ne hp' fun h ↦ Set.disjoint_iff.mp
    (Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes hp)
    ⟨show r ∈ p by simpa [← h] using l.head.2, hr⟩
  refine le_trans ?_ (le_iSup _ ((l.map Subtype.val (fun _ _ ↦ id)).cons p' hp'))
  simp

/-- If `R →+* S` is surjective whose kernel contains a nonzero divisor, then `dim S + 1 ≤ dim R`. -/
lemma ringKrullDim_succ_le_of_surjective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
=== integral dim ===

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.FieldDivision
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.PID

public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.KrullsHeightTheorem

/-!
# Krull dimension of polynomial ring

This file proves properties of the Krull dimension of the polynomial ring over a commutative ring

## Main results

* `Polynomial.ringKrullDim_le`: the Krull dimension of the polynomial ring over a commutative ring
  `R` is less than `2 * (ringKrullDim R) + 1`.

For noetherian rings:
* `Polynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`: the Krull dimension of `R[X]` is `dim R + 1`.
* `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`: the Krull dimension of `R[X₁, ..., Xₙ]` is
  `dim R + n`.
-/

public section

theorem Polynomial.ringKrullDim_le {R : Type*} [CommRing R] :
    ringKrullDim (Polynomial R) ≤ 2 * (ringKrullDim R) + 1 := by
  rw [ringKrullDim, ringKrullDim]
  apply Order.krullDim_le_of_krullDim_preimage_le' (PrimeSpectrum.comap C) ?_ (fun p ↦ ?_)
  · exact fun {a b} h ↦ Ideal.comap_mono h
  · rw [show C = (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)) from rfl, Order.krullDim_eq_of_orderIso
      (PrimeSpectrum.preimageOrderIsoFiber R (Polynomial R) p), ← ringKrullDim,
      ← ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv (polyEquivTensor R (p.asIdeal.ResidueField)).toRingEquiv,
      ← Ring.krullDimLE_iff]

section IsIntegral

variable {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [Algebra.IsIntegral R A]

theorem IsIntegral.comap_lt_comap {I J : Ideal A} [I.IsPrime] (I_lt_J : I < J) :
    I.comap (algebraMap R A) < J.comap (algebraMap R A) :=
  let ⟨I_le_J, x, hxJ, hxI⟩ := SetLike.lt_iff_le_and_exists.mp I_lt_J
  comap_lt_comap_of_integral_mem_sdiff I_le_J ⟨hxJ, hxI⟩ (Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral x)

@[deprecated (since := "2026-05-08")] alias IsIntegralClosure.comap_le_comap :=
  IsIntegral.comap_lt_comap

theorem IsIntegral.isMaximal_of_isMaximal_comap (I : Ideal A) [I.IsPrime]
    (hI : IsMaximal (I.comap (algebraMap R A))) : IsMaximal I :=
  isMaximal_of_isIntegral_of_isMaximal_comap I hI

nontriviality S
  constructor
  intro r
  let p : S[X] := C r * X - 1
  have : (1 : R[X]) ∈ Ideal.span {X} ⊔ (Ideal.span {p}).comap (mapRingHom f) := by
    have H := h _ (isClosed_zeroLocus {p})
    rw [← zeroLocus_span, ← closure_eq_iff_isClosed, closure_image_comap_zeroLocus] at H
    rw [← Ideal.eq_top_iff_one, sup_comm, ← zeroLocus_empty_iff_eq_top, zeroLocus_sup, H]
    suffices ∀ (a : PrimeSpectrum S[X]), p ∈ a.asIdeal → X ∉ a.asIdeal by
      simpa [Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_notMem]
    intro q hpq hXq
    have : 1 ∈ q.asIdeal := by simpa [p] using! (sub_mem (q.asIdeal.mul_mem_left (C r) hXq) hpq)
    exact q.2.ne_top (q.asIdeal.eq_top_iff_one.mpr this)
  obtain ⟨a, b, hb, e⟩ := Ideal.mem_span_singleton_sup.mp this
  obtain ⟨c, hc : b.map (algebraMap R S) = _⟩ := Ideal.mem_span_singleton.mp hb
  refine ⟨b.reverse * X ^ (1 + c.natDegree), ?_, ?_⟩
  · refine Monic.mul ?_ (by simp)
    have h : b.coeff 0 = 1 := by simpa using! congr(($e).coeff 0)
    have : b.natTrailingDegree = 0 := by simp [h]
    rw [Monic.def, reverse_leadingCoeff, trailingCoeff, this, h]
  · have : p.natDegree ≤ 1 := by simpa using! natDegree_linear_le (a := r) (b := -1)
    rw [eval₂_eq_eval_map, reverse, Polynomial.map_mul, ← reflect_map, Polynomial.map_pow,
      map_X, ← revAt_zero (1 + _), ← reflect_monomial,
      ← reflect_mul _ _ natDegree_map_le (by simp), pow_zero, mul_one, hc,
      ← add_assoc, reflect_mul _ _ (this.trans (by simp)) le_rfl,
      eval_mul, reflect_sub, reflect_mul _ _ (by simp) (by simp)]
    simp [← pow_succ']

variable (R S) in
lemma _root_.Algebra.IsIntegral.comap_surjective [Algebra R S] [Algebra.IsIntegral R S]
    [FaithfulSMul R S] :
    Function.Surjective (comap (algebraMap R S)) := by
  intro ⟨p, hp⟩
  have hinj : Function.Injective (algebraMap R S) := FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective _ _
  obtain ⟨Q, _, hQ, rfl⟩ := Ideal.exists_ideal_over_prime_of_isIntegral p (⊥ : Ideal S)
    (by simp [Ideal.comap_bot_of_injective (algebraMap R S) hinj])
  exact ⟨⟨Q, hQ⟩, rfl⟩

lemma _root_.RingHom.IsIntegral.comap_surjective {f : R →+* S} (hf : f.IsIntegral)
    (hinj : Function.Injective f) : Function.Surjective (comap f) := by
  algebraize [f]
  have : FaithfulSMul R S := (faithfulSMul_iff_algebraMap_injective R S).mpr hinj
  exact Algebra.IsIntegral.comap_surjective _ _

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-10")]
alias _root_.RingHom.IsIntegral.specComap_surjective := _root_.RingHom.IsIntegral.comap_surjective

end IsIntegral

data.content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2025 Jingting Wang, Junyan Xu. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Jingting Wang, Junyan Xu, Andrew Yang
-/
module

public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.RingHom
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.TensorProduct
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Quotient
public import Mathlib.Topology.Homeomorph.Lemmas

/-!
# The fiber of a ring homomorphism at a prime ideal

## Main results

* `Ideal.Fiber`: `p.Fiber S` is the fiber of a prime `p` of `R` in an `R`-algebra `S`,
  defined to be `κ(p) ⊗ S`.
* `PrimeSpectrum.preimageHomeomorphFiber` : We show that there is a homeomorphism between the
  fiber of the induced map `PrimeSpectrum S → PrimeSpectrum R` at a prime ideal `p` and
  the prime spectrum of `p.Fiber S`.
-/

@[expose] public section

open Algebra TensorProduct nonZeroDivisors

variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
open IsLocalRing in
instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsLocalRing S] [IsLocalHom (algebraMap R S)] :
    IsLocalRing (ResidueField R ⊗[R] S) :=
  let eSp : ResidueField R ⊗[R] S ≃ₐ[R] S ⧸ (maximalIdeal R).map (algebraMap R S) :=
    (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _).trans
      ((TensorProduct.quotIdealMapEquivTensorQuot S (maximalIdeal R)).symm.restrictScalars _)
  have : Nontrivial (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R ⊗[R] S) := by
    rw [eSp.nontrivial_congr, Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
    exact ((((local_hom_TFAE (algebraMap R S)).out 0 2 rfl rfl).mp inferInstance).trans_lt
      (inferInstance : (maximalIdeal S).IsMaximal).lt_top).ne
  .of_surjective' TensorProduct.includeRight.toRingHom
    (TensorProduct.mk_surjective _ _ _ residue_surjective)

namespace Ideal

lemma ResidueField.exists_smul_eq_tmul_one
    (x : S ⊗[R] p.ResidueField) : ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s, r • x = s ⊗ₜ[R] 1 := by
  obtain ⟨t, r, a, hrt, e⟩ := RingHom.SurjectiveOnStalks.exists_mul_eq_tmul
    p.surjectiveOnStalks_residueField x ⊥ isPrime_bot
  obtain ⟨t, rfl⟩ := IsLocalRing.residue_surjective t
  obtain ⟨⟨y, t⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective p.primeCompl t
  simp only [smul_def, Submodule.mem_bot, mul_eq_zero, algebraMap_residueField_eq_zero,
    IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff, not_or, IsLocalization.AtPrime.mk'_mem_maximal_iff] at hrt
  refine ⟨r * y, p.primeCompl.mul_mem hrt.1 hrt.2, y • a, ?_⟩
  rw [Algebra.smul_def, ← Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight.commutes, smul_tmul,
    ← Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_apply]
  simpa [← tmul_smul, Submonoid.smul_def, ← smul_mul_assoc, smul_comm _ r,
    ← IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, ← algebraMap.coe_smul,
    ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply] using congr(t • $e)

/-- The fiber of a prime `p` of `R` in an `R`-algebra `S`, defined to be `κ(p) ⊗ S`.

See `PrimeSpectrum.preimageHomeomorphFiber` for the homeomorphism between the spectrum of it
and the actual set-theoretic fiber of `PrimeSpectrum S → PrimeSpectrum R` at `p`. -/
abbrev Fiber (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] (S : Type*) [AddCommGroup S] [Module R S] : Type _ :=
  p.ResidueField ⊗[R] S

instance (q : Ideal (p.Fiber S)) [q.IsPrime] : q.LiesOver p :=
  .trans _ (⊥ : Ideal p.ResidueField) _

/-- If `q` is a prime ideal of `p.Fiber S`,  then the localization `(p.Fiber S)_q` is an algebra
over the localization `R_p` since `p.Fiber S` is already an `R_p`-algebra. This `R_p`-algebra
structure on `(p.Fiber S)_q` agrees with the one coming from the fact that `q` lies over `p`. -/
instance (q : Ideal (p.Fiber S)) [q.IsPrime] : Localization.AtPrime.IsLiesOverAlgebra p q where
  algebraMap_eq := (Localization.localRingHom_unique p q _ (Ideal.over_def q p) fun _ ↦ rfl).symm

lemma Fiber.exists_smul_eq_one_tmul (x : p.Fiber S) : ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s, r • x = 1 ⊗ₜ[R] s := by
  obtain ⟨r, hr, s, e⟩ := Ideal.ResidueField.exists_smul_eq_tmul_one _
    (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _ x)
  refine ⟨r, hr, s, by simpa using congr((Algebra.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _).symm $e)⟩

attribute [local instance] Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra in
/-- `p.Fiber S` is isomorphic to the quotient `Sₚ ⧸ pSₚ`. -/
noncomputable def Fiber.algEquivQuotient :
    letI Rp := Localization p.primeCompl
    letI pRp := IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Rp
    letI Sp := Localization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid S p.primeCompl)
    letI pSp := pRp.map (algebraMap Rp Sp)
    p.Fiber S ≃ₐ[S] Sp ⧸ pSp :=
  (commRight R S p.ResidueField).symm.trans <| (tensorQuotientEquiv S _ S _).trans <|
    { __ := Ideal.quotientEquiv _ _ (Localization.tensorLeftAlgEquiv p.primeCompl S) (by
        rw [← Ideal.map_coe includeRight, Ideal.map_map]
        congr
        ext
        simp [Localization.tensorLeftAlgEquiv_apply_one_tmul p.primeCompl])
      commutes' := by simp }

/-- `p.Fiber S` is isomorphic to the quotient `Sₚ ⧸ pSₚ`. -/
noncomputable def Fiber.algEquivAux₁ :
    letI Sp := Localization (algebraMapSubmonoid S p.primeCompl)
    letI pS := p.map (algebraMap R S)
    letI : Algebra S (p.Fiber S) := rightAlgebra
    p.Fiber S ≃ₐ[S] Sp ⧸ pS.map (algebraMap S Sp) :=
  letI : Algebra S (p.Fiber S) := rightAlgebra
  (Fiber.algEquivQuotient p).trans <| quotientEquivAlgOfEq S <| by
    rw [← Localization.AtPrime.map_eq_maximalIdeal, map_map, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq,
      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq R S, ← map_map]

/-- The localization of the fiber `p.Fiber S` is isomorphic to a quotient of a localization. -/
noncomputable def Fiber.algEquivAux₂ (q : Ideal (p.Fiber S)) [q.IsPrime] :
    letI r := q.comap includeRight
    letI Sr := Localization.AtPrime r
    letI pS := p.map (algebraMap R S)
    Localization.AtPrime q ≃ₐ[R] Sr ⧸ pS.map (algebraMap S Sr) :=
  letI : Algebra S (p.Fiber S) := rightAlgebra
  letI Sp := Localization (algebraMapSubmonoid S p.primeCompl)
  letI pS := p.map (algebraMap R S)
  letI SpS := S ⧸ pS
  letI r := q.comap includeRight
